title: COPPA §312.8 — Data security and confidentiality: protective-control evidence for children's data
exclusions: Does NOT substitute for the full regulatory control set; this UC provides one of several evidence streams and must be combined with policy, procedure, and supplemental UCs to demonstrate the complete clause.
evidence: Scheduled search output persisted to the compliance summary index with regulation=COPPA, clause=§312.8, available as a saved search and exportable as a CSV evidence pack for the responsible officer.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for §312.8.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that COPPA §312.8 (Data security and confidentiality) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.50.30: COPPA §312.8 — Data security and confidentiality: protective-control evidence for children's data.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.50.30' running on catalogue-defined data sources (see UC detailedImplementation), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.
compliance.smeCaveat: Phase-C draft: SME review required before uplifting assurance from 'contributing' to 'partial' or 'full'. Confirm that the SPL captures the specific regulator expectation and that the telemetry exists in the deployment.

dataSources: Access logs for child-data repositories, DLP events, encryption-status records, vulnerability scan results.
app: Splunk Enterprise Security, Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform
spl:
index=dlp sourcetype IN (dlp_events, data_access_audit) earliest=-24h
| search data_classification="children_pii" OR data_tag="coppa"
| eval access_authorised=if(action IN ("allowed", "read") AND isnotnull(authorisation_ref), 1, 0)
| eval blocked=if(action IN ("blocked", "denied", "quarantined"), 1, 0)
| stats count AS total_events sum(access_authorised) AS authorised sum(blocked) AS blocked_attempts dc(user) AS distinct_users dc(dest) AS distinct_systems BY system, data_class
| eval unauthorised=total_events - authorised - blocked_attempts
| append [search index=tls sourcetype IN ("stream:tcp", "bro:ssl") dest_port=443 earliest=-24h
  | lookup coppa_data_systems.csv dest OUTPUT coppa_system
  | where coppa_system="true"
  | eval weak_tls=if(ssl_version IN ("SSLv3","TLSv1","TLSv1.0","TLSv1.1"), 1, 0)
  | stats count AS total_conns sum(weak_tls) AS weak_conns BY dest
  | eval system=dest, data_class="encryption_posture", total_events=total_conns, unauthorised=weak_conns, authorised=total_conns-weak_conns, blocked_attempts=0, distinct_users=0, distinct_systems=1]
| where unauthorised>0 OR blocked_attempts>0
| table system, data_class, total_events, authorised, blocked_attempts, unauthorised, distinct_users, distinct_systems
description: Monitors data security and confidentiality for children's PII per COPPA §312.8. Combines DLP monitoring with TLS encryption posture checks on systems storing children's data, addressing both access control and encryption requirements.
value: §312.8 requires reasonable procedures to protect the confidentiality, security, and integrity of children's personal information. Continuous access monitoring and DLP evidence demonstrates active protection.
implementation: (1) Tag data repositories containing children's PII with data_classification=children_pii; (2) ingest DLP and access-audit logs; (3) schedule daily; (4) escalate unauthorised access immediately. Indexes required: `index=access_logs`, `index=dlp`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=data_access_audit`, `sourcetype=dlp_events`.
visualization: Table of flagged records for investigation; single-value KPI of coverage / compliance percentage; time chart of trend over the last 90 days; drill-down per responsible owner. Key fields: `access_authorised`, `authorised`, `blocked`, `blocked_attempts`, `data_class`, `distinct_systems`.
knownFalsePositives: Tuning prerequisites: source field mappings must be in place before the UC will produce reliable metrics; early runs will commonly flag unmapped systems until onboarding is completed.
grandmaExplanation: We watch for monitors access to repositories containing children's personal information and tracks dlp/encryption protective controls, providing evidence of §312.8 data security and confidentiality. This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security, Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Access logs for child-data repositories, DLP events, encryption-status records, vulnerability scan results.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Tag data repositories containing children's PII with data_classification=children_pii; (2) ingest DLP and access-audit logs; (3) schedule daily; (4) escalate unauthorised access immediately.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=dlp sourcetype IN (dlp_events, data_access_audit) earliest=-24h
| search data_classification="children_pii" OR data_tag="coppa"
| eval access_authorised=if(action IN ("allowed", "read") AND isnotnull(authorisation_ref), 1, 0)
| eval blocked=if(action IN ("blocked", "denied", "quarantined"), 1, 0)
| stats count AS total_events sum(access_authorised) AS authorised sum(blocked) AS blocked_attempts dc(user) AS distinct_users dc(dest) AS distinct_systems BY system, data_class
| eval unauthorised=total_events - authorised - blocked_attempts
| append [search index=tls sourcetype IN ("stream:tcp", "bro:ssl") dest_port=443 earliest=-24h
  | lookup coppa_data_systems.csv dest OUTPUT coppa_system
  | where coppa_system="true"
  | eval weak_tls=if(ssl_version IN ("SSLv3","TLSv1","TLSv1.0","TLSv1.1"), 1, 0)
  | stats count AS total_conns sum(weak_tls) AS weak_conns BY dest
  | eval system=dest, data_class="encryption_posture", total_events=total_conns, unauthorised=weak_conns, authorised=total_conns-weak_conns, blocked_attempts=0, distinct_users=0, distinct_systems=1]
| where unauthorised>0 OR blocked_attempts>0
| table system, data_class, total_events, authorised, blocked_attempts, unauthorised, distinct_users, distinct_systems
```

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table of flagged records for investigation; single-value KPI of coverage / compliance percentage; time chart of trend over the last 90 days; drill-down per responsible owner.
